page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 20 shared vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding 3.2 GB of memory per core. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration includes 20 physical cores with a single thread per core, backed by 64 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 caches. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU resources.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 15708.05 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9927.0. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the middle tier, reaching 69.81 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM inference show strong performance, placing in the top 25% of tested configurations.

Given its shared CPU architecture and lack of local storage, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads that require high core counts and moderate memory density without needing dedicated CPU performance. It is well-suited for multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring sustained, non-shared CPU performance.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 20 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Benchmark data reveals a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, landing in the bottom 10%, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics consistently achieve average ratings. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM inference demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. This resource profile provides a cost-effective solution for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and static web serving, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a KVM virtual server with 20 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities.

tagline: A high-core, shared-resource virtual server delivering balanced multi-threaded performance and moderate memory capacity for parallel workloads.

KVM hypervisor on x86_64 architecture with no local storage
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM inference

Best suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and web serving
